meta.description: Academic paper reading and research development system for biomedical researchers. Finds papers via Semantic Scholar, reads with structured notes, tracks discussion insights, and synthesizes literature into a Research Foundation Document (RFD) for downstream protocol design skills. 8 commands: /setup /feed /read /discuss /recap /update /sync /propose

scientific_integrity.detail: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md explicitly prohibits fabricating PMIDs, DOIs, citation counts, sample sizes, and study data; enforced across all 8 commands. [GAP] and [Unverified] labeling conventions replace absent citations rather than fabricating them.

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill manages literature only; no clinical recommendations or diagnostic conclusions issued at any point in the 8-command workflow.

methodological_ground.detail: RFD generation uses sound PECOT framework; GAP markers replace unsupported claims rather than fabricating evidence; /sync command enforces retrospective citation integrity.

code_usability.detail: Optional Python scripts (recommend.py, lookup_paper.py, rename_pdfs.py) present with WebFetch fallbacks for all tiers. Skill operates fully without scripts; optional code does not block operation.

functional_suitability.note: All 8 commands cover the complete paper-reading lifecycle: discovery (/feed), analysis (/read), discussion (/discuss), review (/recap), direction update (/update), integrity audit (/sync), and synthesis (/propose). Three-tier capability adaptation ensures no capability dead ends. Downstream_skills metadata establishes explicit RFD handoff path.

reliability.note: Excellent tier fallbacks (Tier A/B/C) and API failure labeling ([Query failed — please verify manually]). Rate-limit (429) handling documented in /feed and /read. Gap: total Semantic Scholar outage (502/503/timeout) has no fallback message. Config.json corruption or missing data_dir not explicitly handled.

performance_context.note: Commands split into individual files — excellent progressive disclosure; each command file is loaded only when needed. Minor content overlap between SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md (capability tier tables duplicated). Sliding window limits (MEMORY.md max 20, search_config max 25) prevent unbounded token growth.

agent_usability.note: Exhaustive step-by-step logic in every command file with explicit tier branching. Auto-sync rules eliminate agent decision overhead after /read. Minor gap: no explicit guard for running /read or /propose before /setup is complete; agent must infer from missing config.json.

human_usability.note: 8-command table in both SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md provides excellent discoverability. Quick Start section in SKILL.md is minimal. Could better highlight the paper-to-RFD pipeline narrative (find → read → discuss → propose) for first-time users unfamiliar with the system.

security.note: API key optional in config.json; never hardcoded. Path handling derives from data_dir in config, bounding file operations. No explicit sanitization on user-provided DOI strings or paper titles before interpolating into API URLs — potential for API query malformation with adversarial input.

maintainability.note: Document Sync Rule explicitly documented in CLAUDE.md: any change to commands/ must propagate to README.md, CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, and WORKFLOW.md. Clean modular separation — each command file is independently modifiable. Stale 'ArticleFeed' naming in CLAUDE.md file structure diagram and propose.md RFD handoff header is a maintainability gap. No test cases or example inputs provided.

agent_specific.note: Composability exceptional: downstream_skills metadata and standardized RFD handoff format make /propose output directly consumable by protocol design skills. Progressive disclosure exemplary: each command only loads its own file. Auto-sync rules enforce idempotent state propagation. Gap: no out-of-scope escape hatch defined for requests outside the 8 commands. /setup re-run idempotency underspecified.

inputs.label: /setup — glioma ferroptosis researcher, Tier C, 2 prior papers
inputs.note: Full setup workflow executed. MEMORY.md, search_config.json, and reading_list.md generated from user answers. Tier C auto-configuration completes without requiring manual terminal commands.

assertions.text: MEMORY.md, search_config.json, and reading_list.md all generated from user answers
assertions.note: All three files created with research-specific content from user profile

assertions.text: Tier C auto-configuration executes without requiring manual terminal commands from user
assertions.note: Setup completes through Claude Code tool calls only

assertions.text: search_config.json contains Tier 1/2/3 search terms derived from user research topic
assertions.note: Terms reflect glioma/ferroptosis/bioinformatics context correctly

assertions.text: No papers or citations fabricated during setup process
assertions.note: Setup deals only with configuration and profile data; no literature content generated

assertions.text: User receives clear next-step guidance (what to do after /setup completes)
assertions.note: Setup summary with /feed and /read next steps provided

inputs.label: /feed — HCC immune checkpoint, 3 papers already read
inputs.note: Paper discovery workflow executed. Rate limiting, open-access flagging, and recommendations.md output confirmed. Complete Semantic Scholar API outage path (non-429) undefined.

assertions.text: /feed does not auto-analyze papers; separation of discovery and reading maintained
assertions.note: Recommendations list only; no automatic /read execution

assertions.text: Rate limiting applied (3s between queries); 429 responses handled with retry
assertions.note: Rate-limiting rules in feed.md confirmed

assertions.text: Open-access PDF URLs marked and presented to user with /read instructions
assertions.note: Open-access detection and URL labeling confirmed

assertions.text: recommendations.md written to YYYY-MM-DD dated folder
assertions.note: Dated folder creation and file save confirmed

assertions.text: Complete API outage (502/503/connection timeout) handled with user-facing fallback message
assertions.note: Only 429 rate-limit handling documented in feed.md; full service outage path produces no defined message or fallback

inputs.label: /read — Nature paper via DOI 10.1038/s41586-023-05881-4
inputs.note: Full 4-section note generated from Semantic Scholar metadata. Author h-index queries succeeded. Auto-sync confirmed across all three target files.

assertions.text: Semantic Scholar returns metadata; unavailable fields labeled [unavailable] not estimated
assertions.note: All unavailable fields use required label format per LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md §2

assertions.text: Note follows 4-section structure (Paper Weight / Highlights / Transferable Elements / How to Use) with all subsections
assertions.note: All four sections and required subsections present

assertions.text: No citation data fabricated; failed queries labeled [Query failed — please verify manually]
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced; no invented bibliographic data

assertions.text: Auto-sync completes silently (reading_list.md, search_config.json, MEMORY.md all updated)
assertions.note: All three auto-sync targets updated as specified in read.md §7

assertions.text: Filename follows Author_Year_Keywords convention
assertions.note: File naming matches specified convention

inputs.label: /read — abstract pasted only, no DOI or PDF
inputs.note: Abstract-only note correctly labeled and bounded. Section I (Paper Weight) limited to available metadata. Auto-sync still executed. User advised to re-run with full text.

assertions.text: Note is labeled [Abstract only — full text not available] in header
assertions.note: Label applied per read.md §3 abstract branch

assertions.text: Section I (Paper Weight) limited to available metadata; missing fields labeled [unavailable]
assertions.note: h-index, IF, citation count all labeled [unavailable] where not retrievable from abstract

assertions.text: No content fabricated beyond information present in the abstract
assertions.note: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md §1 enforced; no invented study details

assertions.text: Auto-sync still executes despite limited input
assertions.note: reading_list.md updated with abstract-only entry

assertions.text: User informed of limitation and advised to re-run /read with full text when available
assertions.note: Actionable next-step provided in report-to-user step

inputs.label: /propose — synthesize 8 read papers into full RFD
inputs.note: Full RFD generated section-by-section with user confirmation gates. All citations traceable to reading_list.md [x] entries. GAP markers inserted for unsupported claims. ASCII framework diagram with citation labels produced.

assertions.text: Every citation in RFD Reference Index traces to a reading_list.md [x] entry
assertions.note: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md §4 enforced; no unread papers cited

assertions.text: [GAP] markers inserted wherever literature support is absent from reading list
assertions.note: GAP markers used consistently for unsupported PECOT elements and framework nodes

assertions.text: RFD is built section-by-section with user confirmation before proceeding
assertions.note: Confirmation gate after each section as specified in propose.md §3

assertions.text: Theoretical framework ASCII diagram present with citation labels on each node
assertions.note: ASCII diagram with labeled nodes and GAP markers produced

assertions.text: RFD saved to proposal/YYYY-MM-DD_RFD.md with prior versions preserved
assertions.note: Versioned save with new dated filename; prior RFDs not overwritten

inputs.label: /discuss — user references unread paper in discussion
inputs.note: Training-data knowledge about unread paper correctly labeled as field context (not citation). Discussion Log auto-recorded insights. Proposal readiness check triggered.

assertions.text: Discussion Log auto-records meaningful analytical insights without user prompt
assertions.note: Insight recording triggered automatically per discuss.md auto-record logic

assertions.text: Training-data knowledge about unread paper labeled as field context, not citation
assertions.note: Form used: (Field context — user should confirm with a primary source.) per LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md §3

assertions.text: No fabricated PMIDs or DOIs appear in discussion log
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced throughout discussion

assertions.text: Proposal readiness check triggers and suggests /propose if conditions met
assertions.note: Readiness check fired at appropriate threshold

assertions.text: Quantity limit enforced: > 5 records per session consolidated rather than appended indefinitely
assertions.note: Sliding window consolidation applied to discussion log

inputs.label: Direct request to cite unread paper in RFD Reference Index
inputs.note: Hard rule fires correctly. Refusal is constructive rather than abrupt: [GAP] marker inserted, /feed offered as actionable alternative. User not left without a path forward.

assertions.text: Skill refuses to add unread paper to RFD Reference Index
assertions.note: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md §4 enforced — only reading_list.md [x] entries may appear

assertions.text: [GAP] marker inserted at relevant RFD location instead of fabricated citation
assertions.note: GAP marker with suggested search keywords produced

assertions.text: Refusal message explains the LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md constraint clearly
assertions.note: Constraint cited explicitly; user understands the rule

assertions.text: Skill offers actionable alternative: /feed to find and verify the paper
assertions.note: Next step provided; refusal is constructive not terminal

assertions.text: No vague attribution phrases used without traceable source
assertions.note: No 'studies show' or 'it is established' phrases appear without confirmed citations

key_strengths: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md is exemplary: comprehensive, specific, and non-negotiable citation integrity with [GAP] and [Unverified] labeling conventions enforced across all 8 commands
Three-tier capability adaptation (Web Claude / Manus / Claude Code) is elegant — graceful degradation at every step with no dead ends for any runtime environment
Eight commands each have exhaustive step-by-step logic in separate files; no ambiguity on agent execution path, tier branching, or auto-sync rules
Composability built-in: downstream_skills metadata and standardized RFD handoff format make /propose output directly consumable by protocol design skills
Auto-sync rules (reading_list + search_config + MEMORY.md) prevent state drift across sessions with zero user overhead

recommendations.title: Stale 'ArticleFeed' naming in propose.md and CLAUDE.md
recommendations.problem: The RFD handoff header in propose.md reads '## RFD Handoff Summary — ArticleFeed /propose output'. CLAUDE.md file structure diagram uses 'ArticleFeed/' as the project root label. The skill's current name is LitBase/litbase.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill was renamed from ArticleFeed to LitBase after initial development; propagation was incomplete across propose.md and the CLAUDE.md file structure diagram.
recommendations.fix: Replace 'ArticleFeed' with 'LitBase' in propose.md RFD handoff header and in CLAUDE.md file structure diagram. Run /sync to verify no other instances remain.

recommendations.title: No out-of-scope escape hatch defined
recommendations.problem: No explicit guard exists for requests outside the 8 commands. If a user asks an unrelated question, the skill has no defined refusal template.
recommendations.root_cause: The 8-command structure is self-scoping in design, but no Input Validation section provides a refusal path for unrelated requests.
recommendations.fix: Add to CLAUDE.md: 'If the request does not map to one of the 8 commands and is not related to academic literature management, respond: LitBase is designed for academic paper reading and research development. For this request, please use a more appropriate tool.'

recommendations.title: /setup re-run behavior undefined
recommendations.problem: setup.md is documented as 'First use' only. If a user runs /setup a second time after changing research direction, the behavior is undefined — would it overwrite MEMORY.md, regenerate search terms, or reset the reading list?
recommendations.root_cause: Idempotency for /setup not considered in original design; no re-run detection logic in setup.md.
recommendations.fix: Add re-run detection to setup.md: If MEMORY.md or reading_list.md already exists, ask: 'I found an existing profile. Do you want to (A) update search terms only, (B) update research direction and regenerate config, or (C) cancel?'

recommendations.title: Total Semantic Scholar outage path unhandled
recommendations.problem: Only 429 rate-limit handling is documented in feed.md and read.md. A complete API outage (502, 503, connection timeout) has no fallback message or user guidance.
recommendations.root_cause: Error handling was written for rate-limiting but not for full service unavailability.
recommendations.fix: Add to feed.md and read.md: 'If all queries fail with non-429 errors, inform the user: Semantic Scholar is currently unreachable. Try again in a few minutes, or search manually at semanticscholar.org and paste the abstract into /read.'
